mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over 2 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 3 months signals rising inflation expectations that erode purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in rising inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Industrial Production Index over 2 months signals an expansion in supply that reduces prices, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expanding supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the federal funds rate and a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index over 3 months signal a monetary policy mistake that reduces real interest rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in monetary policy mistake periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the Real Estate Sales Index and a 0.5% decrease in the unemployment rate over 2 months signal a strengthening housing market that increases consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 1% and UNRATE falls > 0.5pp in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening housing market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate and a 1% decrease in the Industrial Production Index over 2 months signal a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp and INDPRO falls > 1% in 2 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in weakening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate and a 1% decrease in the Consumer Price Index over 3 months signal a monetary policy mistake that increases real interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp and CPIAUCSL falls > 1% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in monetary policy mistake periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
